options.a: Only the declination of the Sun will affect the duration of civil twilight.
options.b: The declination of the Sun and the latitude of the observer will affect the duration of civil twilight.
options.c: The duration of the civil twilight on 21st of March and on 23rd of September is equal at all places on Earth independent of latitude.
options.d: Civil twilight at the Equator lasts longer than at 60°N or 60°S because the radius of the Equator is larger than the radius of the 60° parallel.
